Chilitepa (Chilita crinita) is a plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and it is a member of the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the citrus family. Chilitepa has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Chilitepa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Chilitepa is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in tincture form, and is also sometimes used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
